capability_objective: The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the case named “Kiosk Login Loop” at “Civic Museum” and the case named “Receipt Export Missing Rows” at “Delta Housing”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use a two-row table with columns Name, ID, Date, and Status; render any absent field as “unknown” and do not infer it.
prompt:
The duty manager needs two customer cases reconciled for shift handoff. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `case-handoff.md` from full case records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `CASES.md` and use the genuine local `./casectl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the case named “Kiosk Login Loop” at “Civic Museum” and the case named “Receipt Export Missing Rows” at “Delta Housing”. Your first case-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes, with no other case operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following case-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./casectl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries. Search summaries are not evidence for dates or statuses.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter case data. Do not expose locations, customer notes, owners, unrelated records, or other extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `case-handoff.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`case-handoff.md` must contain exactly five nonblank lines with no heading, blank lines, or other prose: a Markdown table header, its separator, two data rows in the requested order, and one status-comparison line. Use exactly these table columns: `| Name | ID | Date | Status |`. For a resolved branch, take every displayed value from its successful full-record get and render a missing, null, or empty field as the literal lowercase word `unknown`; do not infer values from the request or search summary. For an unresolved branch, retain its requested name and render its ID, Date, and Status as `unknown`. The final line must be exactly `Status comparison: same (<status>).` when both retrieved statuses are present and equal, `Status comparison: different (<first status> vs <second status>).` when both are present and differ, or `Status comparison: unavailable.` when either status is unavailable. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
